did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper uses deep learning techniques for skin cancer detection. Specifically, it employs Deeplabv3 for segmentation, extracts features using GLCM, GLRM, LBP, and LGP, and utilizes an Ensemble Highest Ranking-based Trio Models (EHR-TM) combining Mobilenet, DenseNet, VGG16, Inception, and Resnet for classification. The Modified Male Commander Selection-based Red Deer Algorithm (MMCS-RDA) is used for hyperparameter optimization.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper presents an original experimental study proposing a new 'Ensemble Highest Ranking-based Trio Models (EHR-TM)' framework for skin cancer detection. It details specific methods including Deeplabv3 for segmentation, feature extraction using GLCM, GLRM, LBP, and LGP, and hyperparameter optimization via the Modified Male Commander Selection-based Red Deer Algorithm (MMCS-RDA). The authors report original performance results, achieving 95% accuracy, and compare these against traditional classifiers, indicating it is a technical study producing new results rather than a review.

title: Optimized ensemble highest ranking-based trio-phased intelligent skin cancer detection: Contribution of deep learning
brief_summary: This study proposes an optimized ensemble highest ranking-based trio-phased intelligent framework for automated skin cancer detection to address challenges in processing difficult lesion features. The methodology employs Deeplabv3 for image segmentation, followed by feature extraction using Gray Level Co-Occurrence Matrix (GLCM), Gray Level Run Length Matrix (GLRM), Local Binary Patterns (LBP), and Local Gradient Patterns (LGP). These features are processed through an Ensemble Highest Ranking-based Trio Models (EHR-TM) combining Mobilenet, DenseNet, VGG16, Inception, and Resnet networks. The hyperparameters for the ensemble classifiers are optimized using a Modified Male Commander Selection-based Red Deer Algorithm (MMCS-RDA). The model was evaluated on standard criterion datasets against traditional classifiers using standard metrics. The proposed approach achieved a superior accuracy of 95%, outperforming individual models such as Inception (91%), Resnet (90%), Mobilenet (86%), VGG16 (85%), and DenseNet (81%).

lesion_types_studied.details: The paper focuses on automated skin cancer detection and skin disorder diagnosis using deep learning. Specific histological lesion types (e.g., melanoma, BCC, nevus) are not explicitly listed in the provided context, only the general category of 'skin cancer' and 'skin disorder' is mentioned.

all_task_types.target: Skin cancer detection / Skin disorder diagnosis
all_task_types.details: The paper proposes a framework for 'automated skin cancer detection' and 'skin disorder diagnosis'. It utilizes an Ensemble Highest Ranking-based Trio Models (EHR-TM) combining Mobilenet, DenseNet, VGG16, Inception, and Resnet for 'categorization' and 'classification'. The performance is evaluated using accuracy (95%), comparing discrete diagnostic outcomes against traditional classifiers.

datasets_used.name: Standard criterion datasets
datasets_used.description: The paper refers to 'standard criterion datasets' and 'criterion datasets' used for skin abnormality images. No specific name (e.g., ISIC, HAM10000) or source is provided in the text.

use.training: Used for the proposed skin diagnosis and categorization framework.
use.testing: Used to test the performance of the designed model against traditional models over standard metrics.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: No hypothesis testing was reported; the paper compares accuracy metrics without statistical significance tests.

standard_criterion_datasets.notes: The proposed approach achieved a superior accuracy of 95% compared to traditional classifiers. The framework performed well and efficiently compared to other conventional systems.

architecture_details.optimization_algorithm: Modified Male Commander Selection-based Red Deer Algorithm (MMCS-RDA)

classification_or_output_head.details: Three-stage classification framework where grades from three classification systems are contemplated for precise skin disorder diagnosis outcomes.

preprocessing_techniques: Segmentation procedure executed by Deeplabv3 to pre-process collected skin abnormality images
conclusions: The proposed Ensemble Highest Ranking-based Trio Models (EHR-TM) framework, which integrates Deeplabv3 for segmentation, feature extraction via GLCM, GLRM, LBP, and LGP, and an ensemble of Mobilenet, DenseNet, VGG16, Inception, and Resnet, achieves a superior accuracy of 95% for skin cancer detection.
The proposed approach outperforms individual traditional classifiers, specifically Inception (91%), Resnet (90%), Mobilenet (86%), VGG16 (85%), and DenseNet (81%).
The integration of the Modified Male Commander Selection-based Red Deer Algorithm (MMCS-RDA) for hyperparameter optimization contributes to the efficiency and performance of the skin diagnosis model.
The proposed skin cancer detection framework performs well and is more efficient than other conventional systems.
important_remarks: The specific name of the 'standard criterion datasets' used for evaluation is not disclosed in the text, preventing verification of data provenance or potential bias.
The paper reports only accuracy metrics (95% for the proposed model) and does not provide other standard classification metrics such as sensitivity, specificity, precision, recall, or F1-score.
No statistical significance testing (e.g., p-values, confidence intervals) is reported to validate the superiority of the proposed model over the baseline classifiers.
The study does not mention external validation on independent datasets, limiting the assessment of generalizability beyond the reported 'standard criterion datasets'.
The abstract contains a typographical error stating the journal volume is '187 (2026)' while the DOI and acceptance date indicate a 2025 publication, which may affect citation accuracy.
